title: potentilla monspeliensis var. labradorica
intro: Potentilla monspeliensis var. labradorica, also known as Tott Hill Cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Potentilla monspeliensis var. labradorica include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to note that its use should be guided by a healthcare professional.
